content_en:
“Why should you be surprised? Don’t you remember that before the Battle of Badr the Prophet 3 had asked what strategy we would adopt in the battle.”

‘Asim bin Thabit % had stood up with his bow in his hand and replied: “When the enemy is a hundred yards away we will use our bows and arrows; when the enemy comes closer we will fight with our spears; when the spears break we will have a hand to hand combat with our swords.”

When the Prophet 3% heard this he said:

“Doubtless this was the way to fight a battle. Whoever wishes to take part in a battle should adopt the strategy of ‘Asim bin Thabit .”

These Companions were just a short distance from Makkah when a group of the Banu Hatheel pursued them and ambushed them. ‘Asim bin Thabit > and his companions drew their swords out of their scabbards and got ready to fight them. Seeing this the Banu Hatheel said:

“Today you cannot fight with us. By god we do not intend to harm you; if you lay down your weapons we swear with Allah as witness that we will not do anything to you.”

The Companions looked at each other on hearing this, as if to consult each other. ‘Asim bin Thabit 4% looked at his companions and said:

“T will not lay down my weapons before the Mushrikeen.”

Then he remembered the announcement of the reward by Salafah bint Sa‘d and he prayed to Allah.

“O Allah! I protect and defend your faith and, have Mercy on me and protect my flesh and my bones; please do not allow them to fall into the hands of the enemy.”

After this supplication he attacked the enemy, with two of his companions joining in with him, They fought bravely and were martyred one by one. The other three surrendered; sure enough the Mushrkeen broke their promise and tied up these men who had surrendered. One of them committed suicide and the other two were sent to Makkah. These two were killed in revenge.

A few hours after the martyrdom of ‘Asim 4, the Quraish learnt of the whereabouts of his body which was lying in the area inhabited by the Banu Hatheel, near Makkah. Their leaders sent a messenger to them asking for the head of ‘Asim bin Thabit 4, so that Salafah bint Sa’d could drink wine from it and cool her temper and slake her thirst for revenge.

The Quraish gave a lot of money to their messenger so that he could give gifts and cash to the Banu Hatheel and succeed in getting the head of “Asim .

When some of the men of the Banu Hatheel went near his pure body to cut off the head they found a whole lot of bees, wasps and hornets surrounding his body and his head. As they neared the body the bees and wasps attacked them, so that they feared for their very lives. They finaily gave up after many attempts, saying to each other.

“Let us wait for the night; in the darkness these dangerous insects will leave the body and only then can we succeed in our aim.”

They went and sat some distance away to wait for nightfall and success in their mission. The sun had not yet set when suddenly dark dense clouds appeared on the sky. The very air seemed to tremble with the thunder. The elders said such heavy rain had never been seen before.

Soon the streets and roads took on the appearance of streams. The low-lying areas were filled, and there was water everywhere as ina flood. It rained heavily the whole night and the Banu Hatheel went back to their homes. Next morning people of the Banu Hatheel came out to get the head of ‘Asim bin Thabit 4s. They went towards the field, but the body was not there. The flood had washed it away to some unknown place. They searched high and iow, but it was nowhere to be found.

The truth was that Allah in His Divine Mercy had answered the supplications of ‘Asim bin Thabit 4 and protected his body. He did not allow his head that was worthy of reverence and respect to fall into the hands of his enemies to be used for such a devilish and sinful act as the drinking of wine. Allah did not allow the Mushrikeen to triumph over the Mu'mineen or true believers; this is how he protects his chosen subjects. The kuffar may plan anything against the Mo'min, but the Divine Plan overcomes all other plans.

“Come back to your Lord ~ well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89: 28)

Zaid bin Harithah 2 The Prophet ES said: “By Allah! Zaid bin Harithah 2s was worthy

of the position. of leadership, and was dearer to me than all others.”

Zaid bin Harithah 2

Sa‘da bint Thu‘labah traveled to meet her family with her son, Zaid bin Harithah. She had just reached the house of a relative on a visit when suddenly bandits of the Banu Qais raided the place and began to loot and plunder. They took away everything they had, stole all their cattle and kidnapped the children. Among these children was Zaid bin Harithah <. At the time he was just eight years old. They took him to the market at ‘Akkadh and sold him to a wealthy chieftain, Hakeem bin Hazam Khawaled, for four hundred dirhams. Hakeem had bought some other slaves as well and took them to Makkah along with him. When Umm-ul-Mo’mineen Khadijah y+ 4) 2, heard of his arrival she went to meet him as he happened to be her nephew.

Some time later Umm-ul-Mo’mineen Khadijah = 4 2, married the Prophet 3s. On this occasion she wished to give a gift to her husband, and she thought Zaid bin Harithah would be the best possible present she could give him. Thus this slave was fortunate enough to be brought up under the tutelage and guidance of the Prophet %, who was an exemplary role model and whose character and behaviour influenced him.

Meanwhile the parents of Zaid bin Harithah 4% were being tormented

by grief. His mother was crying continuously and her heart yearned for her favourite lost son. She was tortured by the uncertainty - was her beloved son alive, and should she wait for him to appear by some miracle? Or was he dead, and should she give up all hopes of ever meeting him again? His father meanwhile, had looked for him in every conceivable place. Had the earth swallowed him or had the heavens zoomed him up?

Sometimes in desperate grief he would start reciting verses which would affect the listeners so as to make them weep. Here are some examples : @ [shed tears for my darling Not knowing what he has undergone Is he alive and should I wait for him? Or have the cruel hands of death taken him? @ By god, I do not know my son What has happened to you Has the soft earth swallowed you Or has the tail hard mountain hidden you? @ The rising sun reminds me of my darling And it again revives memories when it sets in the evening. @ | wander everywhere on the earth, and search for you Mounted on my camel; I will never tire of this wandering. @ Myson my search will continue till I die Eventually all have to die, though false hopes may keep you alive.

One year on the occasion of Hajj some members of the family of Zaid bin Harithah 4 came to Makkah; they were going around the Ka’bah when one of them saw Zaid bin Harithah 4. They recognised him and he too recognised them. They exchanged news about each other’s welfare. When they returned home after performing Hajj, they informed his father that his son was alive and well in Makkah. They told him that they had seen him themselves, and had spoken to him.

As soon as Harithah heard this good news he got his camel ready, took some cash because he thought he may need to buy back his son, and asked his brother Ka‘b to accompany him. Both of them travelled towards Makkah as fast as they could. As soon as they reached Makkah, they went into the presence of the Prophet 3 and said,

“You are a neighbour of the House of Allah and are magnanimous. You feed the poor and assist the helpless. We have come into your presence for our son and for this purpose we have brought a lot of money. For God’s sake have mercy on us; we will be happy to pay you any price you ask for him.”

When the Prophet # heard this he asked,

“Who is the person whose release you are demanding?” They answered, “ Your slave, Zaid bin Harithah .&.” He asked

“Shall I show you a method that will be better than paying a price for him?”

Both of them asked in surprised tones, “What is this method?”

“I will call him here in front you. If he wishes to go with you then he is free to do so. You can take him gladly. I will not take any compensation for him. But on the other hand if he prefers to stay with me then I will not force him to go with you.”

Both the brothers were very happy on hearing this, and said, “Your decision is very just and we accept it.” The Prophet % sent for Zaid bin Harithah and asked him, “Who are these two men?” He answered,

“This is my father Harithah bin Sharjeel, and this is my uncle Ka‘b.”

The Prophet % told him that he had the freedom to either go with them or continue to stay with him.

Without a moment's hesitation he replied:

“T will stay with you.” When his father heard this he said, “This is very sad! Would you rather bea slave than stay with your parents?”
content_id:
"Mengapa kalian harus terkejut? Tidakkah kalian ingat bahwa sebelum Perang Badr, Nabi ﷺ pernah bertanya strategi apa yang akan kita gunakan dalam pertempuran."

'Asim bin Thabit radhiyallahu 'anhu berdiri dengan busurnya di tangan dan menjawab: "Ketika musuh berjarak seratus yard, kita akan menggunakan busur dan anak panah; ketika musuh mendekat, kita akan bertempur dengan tombak; ketika tombak patah, kita akan bertarung jarak dekat dengan pedang."

Ketika Nabi ﷺ mendengar ini, beliau bersabda:

"Tidak diragukan lagi, inilah cara bertempur dalam pertempuran. Siapa pun yang ingin ikut serta dalam pertempuran hendaknya mengikuti strategi 'Asim bin Thabit."

Para sahabat ini baru saja berada dalam jarak dekat dari Makkah ketika sekelompok orang dari Banu Hatheel mengejar dan menyergap mereka. 'Asim bin Thabit radhiyallahu 'anhu dan para sahabatnya mencabut pedang dari sarungnya dan bersiap untuk bertempur. Melihat ini, Banu Hatheel berkata:

"Hari ini kalian tidak perlu melawan kami. Demi Allah, kami tidak bermaksud menyakiti kalian; jika kalian meletakkan senjata, kami bersumpah dengan Allah sebagai saksi bahwa kami tidak akan melakukan apa pun terhadap kalian."

Para sahabat saling memandang satu sama lain mendengar ini, seolah-olah saling bermusyawarah. 'Asim bin Thabit radhiyallahu 'anhu memandang para sahabatnya dan berkata:

"Aku tidak akan meletakkan senjataku di hadapan orang-orang musyrik."

Kemudian dia teringat pengumuman hadiah dari Salafah bint Sa'd dan dia berdoa kepada Allah.

"Ya Allah! Aku melindungi dan membela agama-Mu, maka rahmatilah aku dan lindungilah daging dan tulangku; jangan biarkan mereka jatuh ke tangan musuh."

Setelah du'a ini, dia menyerang musuh, dengan dua orang sahabatnya bergabung bersamanya. Mereka bertempur dengan gagah berani dan gugur sebagai syuhada satu per satu. Tiga orang lainnya menyerah; dan benar saja, orang-orang musyrik mengingkari janji mereka dan mengikat orang-orang yang telah menyerah ini. Salah seorang dari mereka bunuh diri dan dua orang lainnya dikirim ke Makkah. Keduanya dibunuh sebagai balas dendam.

Beberapa jam setelah kesyahidan 'Asim radhiyallahu 'anhu, kaum Quraisy mengetahui keberadaan jenazahnya yang terbaring di wilayah yang dihuni oleh Banu Hatheel, dekat Makkah. Para pemimpin mereka mengirim utusan kepada Banu Hatheel untuk meminta kepala 'Asim bin Thabit radhiyallahu 'anhu, agar Salafah bint Sa'd bisa minum khamr darinya untuk meredakan amarahnya dan memuaskan dahaga balas dendamnya.

Kaum Quraisy memberikan banyak uang kepada utusan mereka agar dia bisa memberikan hadiah dan uang tunai kepada Banu Hatheel dan berhasil mendapatkan kepala 'Asim.

Ketika beberapa orang dari Banu Hatheel mendekati jenazah sucinya untuk memotong kepalanya, mereka menemukan sekumpulan besar lebah, tawon, dan tabuhan mengelilingi tubuh dan kepalanya. Ketika mereka mendekati jenazah itu, lebah dan tawon menyerang mereka, sehingga mereka takut akan keselamatan nyawa mereka sendiri. Mereka akhirnya menyerah setelah banyak percobaan, sambil berkata satu sama lain:

"Mari kita tunggu sampai malam; dalam kegelapan serangga-serangga berbahaya ini akan meninggalkan jenazah dan barulah kita bisa berhasil dalam tujuan kita."

Mereka pergi dan duduk agak jauh untuk menunggu malam tiba dan keberhasilan misi mereka. Matahari belum tenggelam ketika tiba-tiba awan gelap tebal muncul di langit. Udara seolah bergetar oleh guntur. Para tetua berkata hujan sederas itu belum pernah terlihat sebelumnya.

Segera jalan-jalan dan jalanan berubah menjadi seperti sungai. Daerah-daerah rendah tergenang, dan air ada di mana-mana seperti banjir. Hujan turun dengan lebatnya sepanjang malam dan Banu Hatheel kembali ke rumah mereka. Keesokan paginya, orang-orang Banu Hatheel keluar untuk mengambil kepala 'Asim bin Thabit radhiyallahu 'anhu. Mereka menuju ke lapangan, tetapi jenazah itu tidak ada di sana. Banjir telah menghanyutkannya ke tempat yang tidak diketahui. Mereka mencari ke mana-mana, tetapi jenazah itu tidak ditemukan di mana pun.

Kenyataannya adalah bahwa Allah dengan Rahmat Ilahi-Nya telah mengabulkan du'a 'Asim bin Thabit radhiyallahu 'anhu dan melindungi jenazahnya. Allah tidak membiarkan kepalanya yang layak dihormati dan dimuliakan jatuh ke tangan musuh untuk digunakan dalam perbuatan keji dan berdosa seperti minum khamr. Allah tidak membiarkan orang-orang musyrik menang atas kaum mukminin atau para mukmin sejati; beginilah cara Allah melindungi hamba-hamba pilihan-Nya. Orang-orang kafir boleh merencanakan apa pun terhadap seorang mukmin, tetapi Rencana Ilahi mengatasi semua rencana lainnya.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha lagi diridhai-Nya!" (QS. Al-Fajr: 28)

Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Demi Allah! Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu layak

menduduki posisi kepemimpinan, dan dia lebih aku cintai daripada semua yang lain."

Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu

Sa'da bint Thu'labah melakukan perjalanan untuk menemui keluarganya bersama putranya, Zaid bin Harithah. Dia baru saja tiba di rumah seorang kerabat dalam sebuah kunjungan ketika tiba-tiba perampok dari Banu Qais menyerbu tempat itu dan mulai merampok dan menjarah. Mereka mengambil semua yang mereka miliki, mencuri semua ternak mereka dan menculik anak-anak. Di antara anak-anak ini adalah Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu. Saat itu usianya baru delapan tahun. Mereka membawanya ke pasar di 'Akkadh dan menjualnya kepada seorang pemimpin kaya, Hakeem bin Hazam Khawaled, seharga empat ratus dirham. Hakeem juga telah membeli beberapa budak lain dan membawa mereka ke Makkah bersamanya. Ketika Ummul Mukminin Khadijah radhiyallahu 'anha mendengar kedatangannya, beliau pergi menemuinya karena dia kebetulan adalah keponakan beliau.

Beberapa waktu kemudian Ummul Mukminin Khadijah radhiyallahu 'anha menikah dengan Nabi ﷺ. Pada kesempatan ini beliau ingin memberikan hadiah kepada suaminya, dan beliau berpikir Zaid bin Harithah akan menjadi hadiah terbaik yang bisa beliau berikan. Maka budak ini beruntung dibesarkan di bawah bimbingan dan arahan Nabi ﷺ, yang merupakan teladan sempurna dan yang akhlak serta perilakunya sangat memengaruhinya.

Sementara itu, kedua orang tua Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu tersiksa

oleh kesedihan. Ibunya menangis terus-menerus dan hatinya merindukan putra kesayangannya yang hilang. Dia disiksa oleh ketidakpastian — apakah putra tercintanya masih hidup, dan haruskah dia menunggu kemunculannya secara keajaiban? Ataukah dia sudah meninggal, dan haruskah dia melepaskan semua harapan untuk bertemu dengannya lagi? Sementara itu ayahnya telah mencarinya di setiap tempat yang bisa dibayangkan. Apakah bumi telah menelannya atau langit telah mengangkatnya?

Kadang-kadang dalam kesedihan yang mendalam, dia mulai melantunkan syair-syair yang membuat para pendengar menangis. Berikut beberapa contohnya: ● Aku meneteskan air mata untuk kekasihku, Tidak tahu apa yang telah menimpanya. Apakah dia masih hidup dan haruskah aku menunggunya? Atau tangan kejam kematian telah merenggutnya? ● Demi Allah, aku tidak tahu wahai putraku, Apa yang telah terjadi padamu. Apakah bumi yang lembut telah menelanmu, Atau gunung yang keras dan tinggi telah menyembunyikanmu? ● Matahari terbit mengingatkanku pada kekasihku, Dan ia kembali membangkitkan kenangan ketika terbenam di sore hari. ● Aku berkelana ke mana-mana di bumi, dan mencarimu, Di atas untaku; aku tidak akan pernah lelah dari pengembaraan ini. ● Wahai putraku, pencarianku akan terus berlanjut sampai aku mati. Pada akhirnya semua pasti mati, meskipun harapan palsu mungkin membuatmu tetap hidup.

Suatu tahun pada kesempatan haji, beberapa anggota keluarga Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu datang ke Makkah; mereka sedang melakukan tawaf mengelilingi Ka'bah ketika salah seorang dari mereka melihat Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu. Mereka mengenalinya dan dia pun mengenali mereka. Mereka saling bertukar kabar tentang keadaan masing-masing. Ketika mereka pulang setelah menunaikan haji, mereka memberitahu ayahnya bahwa putranya masih hidup dan sehat di Makkah. Mereka mengatakan bahwa mereka telah melihatnya sendiri dan telah berbicara dengannya.

Begitu Harithah mendengar kabar gembira ini, dia menyiapkan untanya, membawa uang tunai karena dia pikir mungkin perlu untuk menebus putranya, dan meminta saudaranya Ka'b untuk menemaninya. Keduanya melakukan perjalanan menuju Makkah secepat mungkin. Begitu mereka tiba di Makkah, mereka menghadap Nabi ﷺ dan berkata,

"Engkau adalah tetangga Baitullah dan engkau dermawan. Engkau memberi makan orang miskin dan membantu orang yang tidak berdaya. Kami datang menghadapmu untuk putra kami dan untuk tujuan ini kami telah membawa banyak uang. Demi Allah, kasihanilah kami; kami akan senang membayar berapapun harga yang engkau minta untuknya."

Ketika Nabi ﷺ mendengar ini, beliau bertanya,

"Siapakah orang yang pembebasannya kalian tuntut?" Mereka menjawab, "Budakmu, Zaid bin Harithah radhiyallahu 'anhu." Beliau bertanya,

"Maukah aku tunjukkan kepada kalian cara yang lebih baik daripada membayar harga untuknya?"

Keduanya bertanya dengan nada terkejut, "Apa metode ini?"

"Aku akan memanggilnya ke sini di hadapan kalian. Jika dia ingin pergi bersama kalian, maka dia bebas melakukannya. Kalian bisa membawanya dengan senang hati. Aku tidak akan meminta kompensasi apa pun atasnya. Namun sebaliknya, jika dia lebih memilih untuk tinggal bersamaku, maka aku tidak akan memaksanya pergi bersama kalian."

Kedua bersaudara itu sangat gembira mendengar hal ini, dan berkata, "Keputusanmu sangat adil dan kami menerimanya." Nabi ﷺ mengutus seseorang untuk memanggil Zaid bin Harithah dan bertanya kepadanya, "Siapa dua orang ini?" Dia menjawab,

"Ini ayahku Harithah bin Syarjeel, dan ini pamanku Ka'b."

Nabi ﷺ memberitahunya bahwa dia memiliki kebebasan untuk pergi bersama mereka atau tetap tinggal bersamanya.

Tanpa ragu sesaat pun dia menjawab:

"Aku akan tetap tinggal bersamamu." Ketika ayahnya mendengar ini, dia berkata, "Ini sangat menyedihkan! Apakah kamu lebih memilih menjadi budak daripada tinggal bersama orang tuamu?"
content_ar: “Why should you be surprised? Don’t you remember that قبل the المعركة of Badr النبي ﷺ 3 had asked what strategy we would adopt in the المعركة.” ‘Asim bin Thabit % had stood up with his bow in his hand and replied: “عندما the العدو is a hundred yards away we will use our bows and arrows; عندما the العدو comes closer we will fight with our spears; عندما the spears break we will have a hand to hand combat with our السيفs.” عندما النبي ﷺ 3% heard this قال: “Doubtless this was the الطريق to fight a المعركة. Whoever wishes to take part in a المعركة should adopt the strategy of ‘Asim bin Thabit .” These الصحابة were just a short distance from مكة المكرمة عندما a group of the Banu Hatheel pursued them and ambushed them. ‘Asim bin Thabit > and his الصحابة drew their السيفs out of their scabbards and got ready to fight them. Seeing this the Banu Hatheel said: “Today you cannot fight with us. By god we do not intend to harm you; إذا you lay down your weapons we swear with الله as witness that we will not do anything to you.” The الصحابة looked at بعضهم بعضاً on hearing this, as إذا to consult بعضهم بعضاً. ‘Asim bin Thabit 4% looked at his الصحابة and said: “T will not lay down my weapons قبل the Mushrikeen.” Then he remembered the announcement of the الأجر by Salafah bint Sa‘d and he prayed to الله. “O الله! I protect and defend your الإيمان and, have الرحمة on me and protect my flesh and my bones; please do not allow them to fall into the hands of the العدو.” بعد this الدعاء he attacked the العدو, with two of his الصحابة joining in with him, They قاتل ببسالة and were الشهيدed one by one. The other three surrendered; sure enough the Mushrkeen broke their promise and tied up these men who had surrendered. One of them committed suicide and the other two were sent to مكة المكرمة. These two were killed in revenge. A few hours بعد the الشهيدdom of ‘Asim 4, the Quraish learnt of the whereabouts of his الجسد which was lying in the area inhabited by the Banu Hatheel, near مكة المكرمة. Their leaders sent a messenger to them asking for the head of ‘Asim bin Thabit 4, لذا that Salafah bint Sa’d could drink wine from it and cool her temper and slake her thirst for revenge. The Quraish gave a lot of المال to their messenger لذا that he could give gifts and cash to the Banu Hatheel and succeed in getting the head of “Asim . عندما some of the men of the Banu Hatheel went near his pure الجسد to cut off the head they found a whole lot of bees, wasps and hornets surrounding his الجسد and his head. As they neared the الجسد the bees and wasps attacked them, لذا that they feared for their very lives. They finaily gave up بعد many attempts, saying to بعضهم بعضاً. “Let us wait for the night; in the darkness these dangerous insects will leave the الجسد and only then can we succeed in our aim.” They went and sat some distance away to wait for nightfall and الفلاح in their mission. The sun had not yet set عندما suddenly dark dense clouds appeared on the sky. The very air seemed to tremble with the thunder. The elders said such heavy rain had never been seen قبل. Soon the streets and roads took on the appearance of streams. The low-lying areas were filled, and there was water everywhere as ina flood. It rained heavily the whole night and the Banu Hatheel went back to their homes. Next morning people of the Banu Hatheel came out to get the head of ‘Asim bin Thabit 4s. They went towards the field, but the الجسد was not there. The flood had washed it away to some unknown place. They searched high and iow, but it was nowhere to be found. The الحق was that الله in His Divine الرحمة had answered the supplications of ‘Asim bin Thabit 4 and protected his الجسد. He did not allow his head that was worthy of reverence and الاحترام to fall into the hands of his enemies to be used for such a devilish and sinful act as the drinking of wine. الله did not allow the Mushrikeen to triumph over the Mu'mineen or true المؤمنون; this is how he protects his chosen subjects. The kuffar may plan anything against the Mo'min, but the Divine Plan overcomes all other plans. “Come back to your Lord ~ well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89: 28) Zaid bin Harithah 2 النبي ﷺ ES said: “By الله! Zaid bin Harithah 2s was worthy of the position. of leadership, and was dearer to me than all others.” Zaid bin Harithah 2 Sa‘da bint Thu‘labah traveled to meet her الأسرة with her son, Zaid bin Harithah. She had just reached the house of a relative on a visit عندما suddenly bandits of the Banu Qais raided the place and began to loot and plunder. They took away everything they had, stole all their cattle and kidnapped the الأولاد. Among these الأولاد was Zaid bin Harithah <. At the time he was just eight years old. They took him to the market at ‘Akkadh and sold him to a wealthy chieftain, Hakeem bin Hazam Khawaled, for four hundred dirhams. Hakeem had bought some other slaves as well and took them to مكة المكرمة along with him. عندما Umm-ul-Mo’mineen Khadijah y+ 4) 2, heard of his arrival she went to meet him as he happened to be her nephew. Some time later Umm-ul-Mo’mineen Khadijah = 4 2, married النبي ﷺ 3s. On this occasion she wished to give a الهبة to her الزوج, and she thought Zaid bin Harithah would be the best possible present she could give him. وهكذا this slave wأماtunate enough to be brought up under the tutelage and الهداية of النبي ﷺ %, who was an exemplary role model and whose الأخلاق and السلوك influenced him. Meanwhile the الوالدان of Zaid bin Harithah 4% were being tormented by الحزن. His الأم was crying continuously and her القلب yearned for her favourite lost son. She was tortured by the uncertainty - was her beloved son alive, and should she wait for him to appear by some miracle? Or was he dead, and should she give up all hopes of ever meeting him again? His الأب meanwhile, had looked for him in every conceivable place. Had the earth swallowed him or had the heavens zoomed him up? Sometimes in desperate الحزن he would start reciting الآيات which would affect the listeners لذا as to make them weep. Here are some examples : @ [shed tears for my darling Not knowing what he has undergone Is he alive and should I wait for him? Or have the cruel hands of الموت taken him? @ By god, I do not know my son What has happened to you Has the soft earth swallowed you Or has the tail hard mountain hidden you? @ The rising sun reminds me of my darling And it again revives memories عندما it sets in the evening. @ | wander everywhere on the earth, and search for you Mounted on my camel; I will never tire of this wandering. @ Myson my search will continue till I die Eventually all have to die, though false hopes may keep you alive. One year on the occasion of الحج some members of the الأسرة of Zaid bin Harithah 4 came to مكة المكرمة; they were going around the Ka’bah عندما one of them saw Zaid bin Harithah 4. They recognised him and he أيضاً recognised them. They exchanged news about بعضهم بعضاً’s welfare. عندما they returned home بعد performing الحج, they informed his الأب that his son was alive and well in مكة المكرمة. They told him that they had seen him themselves, and had spoken to him. As soon as Harithah heard this الخير news he got his camel ready, took some cash لأن he thought he may need to buy back his son, and asked his الأخ Ka‘b to accompany him. Both of them travelled towards مكة المكرمة as fast as they could. As soon as they reached مكة المكرمة, they went into the presence of النبي ﷺ 3 and said, “You are a neighbour of the House of الله and are magnanimous. You feed the poor and assist the helpless. We have come into your presence for our son and for this purpose we have brought a lot of المال. For God’s sake have الرحمة on us; we will be happy to pay you any price you ask for him.” عندما النبي ﷺ # heard this he asked, “Who is the person whose release you are demanding?” They answered, “ Your slave, Zaid bin Harithah .&.” He asked “Shall I show you a method that will be better than paying a price for him?” Both of them asked in surprised tones, “What is this method?” “I will call him here in front you. إذا he wishes to go with you then he is free to do لذا. You can take him gladly. I will not take any compensation for him. But من ناحية أخرى إذا he prefers to stay with me then I will not force him to go with you.” Both the brothers were very happy on hearing this, and said, “Your decision is very just and we accept it.” النبي ﷺ % sent for Zaid bin Harithah and asked him, “Who are these two men?” رد, “This is my الأب Harithah bin Sharjeel, and this is my uncle Ka‘b.” النبي ﷺ % told him that he had the freedom to either go with them or continue to stay with him. Without a moment's hesitation أجاب: “T will stay with you.” عندما his الأب heard this قال, “This is very sad! Would you rather bea slave than stay with your الوالدان?”
